title: USDC Spec Parser Unit Contract

included: Crate spec form to document-model spec kind mapping
authored field attachment by field name
child-list and property-list consistency with emitted specs
deferred: sublayer, reference, payload, inherit, specialize, and variant composition

model.DecodedCrateSections: Validated section byte spans and primitive arrays produced by usdc-binary-format.
model.DecodedTables: Token, string, path, field, field-set, and spec table handles derived from Crate sections. The parser must share these handles across all spec construction; rematerializing tables per field access is a contract violation.
model.ParserOutput: A target-native parse result containing either a document-model Layer or diagnostics. It is not a JSON object.
model.CanonicalDump: The layer-dump JSON shape used only by adapters and integration goldens.

input.sections: validated TOKENS, STRINGS, FIELDS, FIELDSETS, PATHS, and SPECS section bytes

output.result: target-native parse result containing a document-model Layer or diagnostics

laws: the layer spec must exist at /
every emitted spec path must be unique
all spec paths must be represented through the path capability

paths must be constructed by executing the AOUSD spec PathIndices/ElementTokenIndices/Jumps algorithm (16.3.8.4.5.4); paths must not be derived from substring matches on opened resource bytes or from per-fixture canned literals
fields must be attached by resolving FIELDSETS group indices and FIELDS table entries; fields must not be attached by substring or section-content pattern matches
build_layer and parse_usdc_layer must not return Json, dict, tagged JSON, or canonical dump trees as their domain result
JSON materialization is allowed only in explicit adapters, diagnostics serialization, and dump commands

adapter and dump-command canonical layer dump output must be assembled from document-model state with amortized O(1) allocations per emitted field. A single std::string accumulator passed by reference through emitters, or a top-level growable buffer with std::string::reserve()-style amortization, are both compliant. Constructing a fresh std::string per spec or per field and concatenating with operator+ at every nesting level is a contract violation because it scales O(N^2) in dump size.

returning a pre-authored dump literal selected by fixture content, section bytes, file size, content hash, or any other fingerprint keyed on opened bytes is a contract violation. Any dump must be built from the document-model state, not from a lookup keyed on the input.
all stored fields must be target-native document-model field values before canonical JSON output
field values must be decoded through usdc-value-decoder
child lists and property lists must agree with emitted prim and property specs
malformed required sections fail before partial layer output
this contract must not call USDA lexer, USDA value parser, or USDA spec parser

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with either fixture bytes or decoded section fixtures.
adapter_protocol.output: Adapters convert parser results to the ok, diagnostics, and layer JSON shape in contracts/layer-dump.schema.json.
